hypothesis: Systems-optimizer recovery becomes safer if runner-stability cards must include at least one replayable runner script command and cannot rely on `node CLAW/scripts/run-site-script.mjs claw:health` alone as evidence.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` accepted runner-stability cards whose `evidence_commands` only named `node CLAW/scripts/run-site-script.mjs claw:health`, so a passive health check could masquerade as replayable recovery proof. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects any runner-stability card that omits `node CLAW/scripts/recover-cycle.mjs` or `node CLAW/scripts/run-recovery-drill.mjs`, while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if runner-stability cards must carry at least one replayable runner script command instead of health-only evidence and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Runner-stability evidence must include at least one replayable runner script command; `claw:health` alone is observability, not recovery proof.
